pi_items.source: Task definition - valid GeoGebra worksheet filename format (.ggb is standard GeoGebra format)
Geographic knowledge - reasonable tolerance for identifying city locations; all three coordinates are within this tolerance

Web search on Chaos Game/Sierpinski Triangle: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chaos_game, https://beltoforion.de/en/recreational_mathematics/chaos_game.php. Sources confirm 'few hundred' points creates sufficient detail for visualization; 500 points well exceeds minimum requirements

privileged_info_summary: The chaos_game_fractal task uses three European capital cities as vertices for a Sierpinski Triangle visualization. All coordinate claims are verified: London at approximately [-0.13, 51.51], Paris at approximately [2.35, 48.85], and Brussels at approximately [4.35, 50.85]. All coordinates are within the stated tolerance of 0.5 degrees (~55-60 km). The task requires generating at least 500 points using the Chaos Game algorithm, which is a well-established minimum for visualizing the Sierpinski Triangle fractal pattern. The output is saved as 'chaos_europe.ggb', a standard GeoGebra worksheet file format.
